mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales levels relative to their 12-month trend indicate resilient household demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is above 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate and decrease in nonfarm payrolls signals a contracting labor market that reduces aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to significantly predict negative returns (interaction term p-value > 0.05) in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5% during a monetary tightening cycle, the central bank maintains restrictive real rates that raise discount rates and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5% during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum reflects real-time manufacturing output strength; elevated production levels drive short-term equity optimism and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Mean forward 5-day return following high INDPRO readings is not significantly different from zero in a t-test (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signal weakness in residential investment and construction employment, foreshadowing a broader demand slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,200 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate combined with decelerating retail sales forces a profit squeeze through elevated financing costs and weakening top-line growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS does not significantly predict negative returns (t-stat < 2.0) in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.
